title: gastrolobium heterophyllum
intro: Gastrolobium heterophyllum, also known as poison, is a plant species native to Western Australia, containing toxic compounds such as glycosides and alkaloids that can be hazardous to humans and animals if ingested. However, in small amounts, the plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Gastrolobium heterophyllum include its potential to treat various conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to exercise caution when handling and consuming Gastrolobium heterophyllum due to its toxic nature.
